title: 1.1: Identifies that the transformation has an emptyValue option defaulting to SpecialValue.Empty (e.g., DEFAULT_EMPTY_VALUE = SpecialValue.Empty)
1.2: Shows that SpecialValue.Empty results in empty string '' (e.g., getSpecialValue returns '' for Empty case)
1.3: Identifies that bar charts convert non-numeric values to null using Number.isFinite (e.g., mapping non-finite values to null)
1.4: States that null values in chart data mean no data point or absent bar rather than zero-height bar
1.5: Provides comparison or list showing how 0, '', and null are treated differently by Number.isFinite (e.g., 0 is finite, '' and null are not)
1.6: States that there is no SpecialValue option that produces numeric zero
1.7: Shows concrete example of semantic difference for calculations like averages where count of values differs (e.g., average of two values vs average of three values including zero)

1.8: Provides a concrete sparse dataset example showing row/column/value input and resulting matrix output with missing value filled (e.g., input with three data points resulting in fourth cell being empty string)
2.1: Claims the transformation produces numeric zero for missing intersections by default
2.2: Claims that setting emptyValue to SpecialValue.Null will make bars render at zero height
